articles.title: FIGJAM DIAGRAMMING IN CLAUDE
articles.summary: Claude now supports FigJam diagramming through the Figma connector, allowing users to transform conversations, documents, and images into visual diagrams, including flowcharts, state diagrams, Gantt charts, and sequence diagrams. This feature helps break down complexity and visualize processes, system states, project timelines, and workflows. Generated diagrams can be opened in FigJam for refinement and team collaboration.

articles.title: INTRODUCING GOOGLE ADS FOR WEBFLOW: THE AI-DRIVEN WAY TO GROW SMARTER AND FASTER
articles.summary: Webflow has launched a Google Ads integration that allows marketing teams to create, manage, and optimize ad campaigns directly within the Webflow platform, eliminating the need to switch between tools. The integration leverages Google AI and Performance Max to help teams reach high-intent audiences across Search, YouTube, Display, and other Google properties while maintaining seamless brand consistency from ad to landing page. Early adopters, including Fried Egg Golf, You.com, and Proctor + Stevenson, report faster campaign launches and better alignment between advertising strategy and website experiences.

articles.title: KOTO EVOLVES GOFUNDME'S BRAND AS PLATFORM EXPANDS BEYOND INDIVIDUAL GIVING
articles.summary: Global creative studio Koto has partnered with GoFundMe on a brand evolution that reflects its expansion from peer-to-peer fundraising into a broader giving ecosystem, while preserving the platform's emotional clarity and trust. Centered on the reimagined Progress Circle and supported by refined logo details, expanded colour and type systems, and updated imagery, motion, and voice, the new identity uses a single, flexible idea to create coherence across products, audiences, and experiences as GoFundMe continues to scale.

articles.title: FRICTION BY DESIGN
articles.summary: Frictionless digital design optimizes for speed and efficiency, but removing all obstacles can eliminate awareness and meaningful decision-making. Deliberately designed friction—like confirmation pauses or undo delays—create moments of attention that respect human judgment rather than pushing machine-speed interactions. Good friction is intentional and purposeful, while bad friction wastes time without delivering value.

articles.title: HOW TO FAVICON IN 2026: THREE FILES THAT FIT MOST NEEDS
articles.summary: Modern websites need only three favicon files instead of the typical 20+ icons: a 32×32 ICO for legacy browsers, an SVG with light/dark theme support for modern browsers, and a 180×180 PNG for Apple devices. Progressive Web Apps require two additional PNG files (192×192 and 512×512) referenced in a web manifest JSON file. This minimalist approach relies on SVG scalability and browser downscaling rather than multiple static files, eliminating outdated formats like Windows Tile icons and Safari's pinned tab icons while maintaining compatibility across all major browsers and devices.

articles.title: PHOTOSHOP JUST FIXED SOME OF ITS MOST ANNOYING WORKFLOW PROBLEMS
articles.summary: Adobe's latest Adobe Photoshop update focuses on reducing everyday friction for working creatives, introducing proper non-destructive, maskable adjustment layers for Clarity, Dehaze, and Grain alongside quieter but meaningful workflow improvements. Upgraded Firefly-powered generative tools, more reliable reference image handling, and a new Dynamic Text beta collectively make edits cleaner, more flexible, and easier to trust in real production work without fundamentally changing how Photoshop is used.

articles.title: TURN IDEAS INTO FULLY FUNCTIONAL APPS
articles.summary: With JDoodle AI, you can validate an idea, build a working prototype, and share it all without needing a developer or writing any code.

articles.title: AI USER RESEARCH AND INTERVIEWS PLATFORM
articles.summary: Articos is the AI-first user research and interviews platform that delivers human-like insights in 30 minutes - powered by virtual users.

articles.title: AI VIDEO GENERATOR, TEXT, AND IMAGE TO VIDEO
articles.summary: Generate stunning AI videos from text prompts or images in seconds. Create HD videos for social media, ads, and more.

articles.title: ATOMIC DESIGN: FROM UI COMPOSITION TO REAL-WORLD APPLICATIONS
articles.summary: Atomic Design effectively organizes UI components into atoms, molecules, and organisms, but it should serve as a composition pattern rather than a complete application architecture. When components accumulate business logic, data fetching, and state management, they become difficult to test and maintain, turning the UI into a monolith. The proposed solution separates UI composition (where Atomic Design lives) from application architecture through a feature-driven organization, with a strict rule that prevents UI components from importing domain logic or accessing business state.

articles.title: HIRUKI: A DESIGN HOUSE BETTING ON EMOTION IN AN ERA OF SYSTEMS AND SCALE
articles.summary: HIRUKI is a newly launched design house founded by designer Julen, drawing on his experience at studios and teams including R/GA, Collins, U.N.N.A.M.E.D., Google Creative Lab, and Apple Marcom Design, and positioning itself as a collective that treats brand design as something to be felt rather than optimised. Built around small, agile teams and a philosophy rooted in emotion, context, and craft, HIRUKI prioritizes depth over speed, collaborative authorship over rigid structures, and intentional growth over scale, aiming to create brand experiences that reveal meaning, coherence, and feeling over time.

articles.title: DEFY REJECTS BLACK-AND-WHITE MINIMALISM FOR GERALD'S MENSWEAR BRAND
articles.summary: The launch of Gerald, designed by Defy for heritage retailer Boyds, focused on inheriting standards rather than copying luxury signals, creating an affordable menswear brand that feels confident, restrained, and authentic to visually literate men aged 25–40. Led by Executive Creative Director Nik Greenblatt, the identity balances classic typography with modern discipline, a craft-rooted colour palette drawn from tailoring materials, and carefully considered physical touchpoints, positioning Gerald as an intelligent entry point into the Boyds ecosystem that has resonated strongly with customers since launch.

articles.title: FLORA LAUNCHES NODE-BASED DESIGN TOOL TO UNIFY CREATIVE AI MODELS AND RAISES $42M
articles.summary: FLORA unifies the best creative AI models across text, image, and video into one workflow to accelerate the creative process.

articles.title: THIS AI CREATIVITY STUDY SAYS YOU STILL BEAT IT, IF YOU'RE TOP TIER
articles.summary: A large-scale study comparing over 100,000 humans with leading AI models on creativity tasks found that some systems now outscore average participants on standardized divergent thinking tests.

articles.title: HOW SMART WEARABLES ARE CHANGING THE WAY DESIGNERS TRACK PRODUCTIVITY
articles.summary: Smart wearables like rings and fitness trackers are becoming essential tools for designers to monitor work habits, time management, and efficiency in creative processes by providing real-time data on movement, focus, and mood.
